title: Do-While Loop
description: A variant that always executes the code at least once.

slides.title: The do-while Loop 🔄
slides.content:
The `do/while` loop is a variant of the `while` loop.

This loop will execute the code block **once, before** checking if the condition is true, then it will repeat the loop as long as the condition is true.

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Watch what happens even if the condition is false from the start.

quizData.question: How many times does the loop above run?
quizData.explanation: Because it's a do-while loop, it "does" the code first. It prints 0, increments i to 1, then checks the while condition (1 < -5), which is false, so it stops. It ran exactly once.

slides.title: When to use it? 🤔
slides.content:
Use a `do-while` loop when you want to show a menu to a user, ask for input, and then loop ONLY if the input is incorrect.

You need to show the menu at least once!

quizData.question: Like standard while loops, you must end do-while statements with a semicolon after the while condition.
quizData.explanation: The syntax is do { ... } while (condition); Notice the semicolon at the end of the while!
